title: tephroseris integrifolia subsp. primulifolia
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. primulifolia, also known as the primrose-leaved tephroseris, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of the Himalayas and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. primulifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
